excerpt: The vastness of the heavens is boundless and infinite; it constitutes the dwelling place of the Holy Spirits. What we call circumference denotes that which cannot be encompassed by measure, yet it also refers to the one who encompasses all things—and this encompassing reality belongs to Allāh alone.
source_location_hint: treatise on cosmology and divine unity

excerpt: The spiritual ascent and descent of souls occurs through four distinct states: emanation (Ibdāʿ), resurgence (Qiyāma), the reversal (Hawl), and the face or orientation (Wajh), wherein the light is drawn from the material sphere and restored to the spiritual realm.
source_work: Rahat al-ʿAql (The Repose of the Intellect)

excerpt: The Prophet taught that jihād is made obligatory upon three dimensions: the heart's sincere faith, the tongue's confession, and the body's righteous action. A mujtahid is one who subdues the soul and renounces all that contradicts the commandments of the higher intellect.
source_location_hint: section on jihād and spiritual struggle

excerpt: The letters of Allāh in numerical value yield: Alif (1, the sustainer), first Lām (30, three prophets of ten forms each), second Lām (30, three prophets of ten forms each), and Hā (5, the Five Pure Ones), together manifesting the twelve in every cycle.

Allāh is the supreme name, yet the true name in its innermost reality is the First Intellect (al-ʿAql al-Awwal), the first source and origin from which all creation unfolds and all other names derive.
